questions.stem: This tissue biopsy shows a large, thick-walled round structure filled with numerous small internal bodies. This finding is essentially pathognomonic for which fungal infection?

explanation.summary: Giant thick-walled spherules filled with endospores are the diagnostic tissue form of Coccidioides.
explanation.whyCorrect: Giant thick-walled spherules filled with endospores are the diagnostic tissue form of Coccidioides.

questions.stem: These chest images (radiograph A and CT B) from a patient in an endemic river-valley region show a nodular pattern mimicking miliary tuberculosis. Which infection should be suspected?

explanation.summary: Acute pulmonary histoplasmosis can produce a nodular pneumonia with adenopathy resembling miliary TB; imaging alone is not diagnostic and requires laboratory confirmation.
explanation.whyCorrect: Acute pulmonary histoplasmosis can produce a nodular pneumonia with adenopathy resembling miliary TB; imaging alone is not diagnostic and requires laboratory confirmation.

questions.stem: This chest radiograph shows cavitary lesions (dark areas within white opacities) in a smoker with chronic pulmonary disease. This cavitary appearance most closely mimics which condition?

explanation.summary: Cavitation and adenopathy in chronic pulmonary histoplasmosis can closely mimic tuberculosis, so identification and ruling out other causes is essential.
explanation.whyCorrect: Cavitation and adenopathy in chronic pulmonary histoplasmosis can closely mimic tuberculosis, so identification and ruling out other causes is essential.

questions.stem: This microscopy shows very small yeast cells with narrow-based budding, some resembling an hourglass or infinity sign as they divide. Which organism does this morphology indicate?

explanation.summary: Tiny (2-5 um) yeasts with narrow-based budding are characteristic of Histoplasma; Blastomyces shows broad-based budding and Cryptococcus has a wide capsule.
explanation.whyCorrect: Tiny (2-5 um) yeasts with narrow-based budding are characteristic of Histoplasma; Blastomyces shows broad-based budding and Cryptococcus has a wide capsule.

questions.stem: This mold form (grown at 25 C) shows large tuberculate structures alongside much smaller conidia (arrows). This combination is diagnostic of which fungus?

explanation.summary: The mold form of Histoplasma has both microconidia and distinctive large macroconidia (giant suns next to tiny microconidia); Blastomyces has conidia but no macroconidia.
explanation.whyCorrect: The mold form of Histoplasma has both microconidia and distinctive large macroconidia (giant suns next to tiny microconidia); Blastomyces has conidia but no macroconidia.

questions.stem: This mold-form microscopy shows alternating barrel-shaped units resembling a bracelet. Which organism is this?

explanation.summary: The mold form of Coccidioides produces alternating barrel-shaped arthroconidia with clear cells between them, giving a bracelet-like appearance.
explanation.whyCorrect: The mold form of Coccidioides produces alternating barrel-shaped arthroconidia with clear cells between them, giving a bracelet-like appearance.

questions.stem: In this immunodiffusion plate the central well contains antigen, surrounded by control and test-serum wells. A cloudy line forming between a test-serum well and the central well indicates which result for that sample?

explanation.summary: A precipitin line between a test well and the antigen well signals antigen-antibody complex formation, a positive result; wells that pass by one another without a line are negative.
explanation.whyCorrect: A precipitin line between a test well and the antigen well signals antigen-antibody complex formation, a positive result; wells that pass by one another without a line are negative.

questions.stem: This microscopy shows yeast cells each surrounded by a large clear zone (an unstained halo). Which structural feature accounts for this appearance?

explanation.summary: Cryptococcus has a very large polysaccharide capsule that excludes stain, creating a clear halo around the yeast.
explanation.whyCorrect: Cryptococcus has a very large polysaccharide capsule that excludes stain, creating a clear halo around the yeast.

questions.stem: These brain MRIs from a 64-year-old show space-occupying nodular lesions (arrows). Among the endemic fungi, which is the most likely cause of this CNS involvement (meningitis and mass-like cryptococcomas)?

explanation.summary: Although all these fungi can disseminate to the CNS, Cryptococcus is by far the most likely to infect the central nervous system, causing meningitis and cryptococcomas.
explanation.whyCorrect: Although all these fungi can disseminate to the CNS, Cryptococcus is by far the most likely to infect the central nervous system, causing meningitis and cryptococcomas.

questions.stem: This CSF preparation uses a negative stain that leaves a clear halo around each yeast against a dark background. Which staining method and organism does this represent?

choices.label: C
choices.text: India ink stain highlighting the capsule of Cryptococcus

explanation.summary: India ink is a classic negative stain for CSF: the thick capsule of Cryptococcus excludes the ink, producing a clear halo around each yeast on a dark background.
explanation.whyCorrect: India ink is a classic negative stain for CSF: the thick capsule of Cryptococcus excludes the ink, producing a clear halo around each yeast on a dark background.

questions.stem: This severely immunocompromised (AIDS) patient presents with widespread cutaneous papules and nodules across the trunk. Given profound T-cell deficiency, disseminated infection by which endemic fungus is most likely?

explanation.summary: Disseminated histoplasmosis spreads via the reticuloendothelial system in patients with severe immunosuppression and can seed the skin among many organ systems.
explanation.whyCorrect: Disseminated histoplasmosis spreads via the reticuloendothelial system in patients with severe immunosuppression and can seed the skin among many organ systems.

questions.stem: A patient with coccidioidomycosis develops these tender, red nodules on the shins. This finding most likely represents which of the following?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A hypersensitivity reaction to amphotericin B

choices.label: B
choices.text: An immune (hypersensitivity) reaction to circulating fungal antigens, not tissue dissemination

choices.label: C
choices.text: Direct fungal dissemination to the skin

explanation.summary: Erythema nodosum reflects the immune system's response to fungal antigens leaking into the blood, not spread of the organism to the skin; it can also occur with non-infectious causes such as Crohn disease.
explanation.whyCorrect: Erythema nodosum reflects the immune system's response to fungal antigens leaking into the blood, not spread of the organism to the skin; it can also occur with non-infectious causes such as Crohn disease.

questions.stem: A dimorphic fungus with the endemic range shown here (overlapping Histoplasma but lacking any bird/bat-dropping association) classically forms which yeast morphology in tissue?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Encapsulated yeast with an India-ink halo

explanation.summary: This is the range of Blastomyces, which favors decomposing leaves/wood and forms large yeast with characteristic broad-based budding.
explanation.whyCorrect: This is the range of Blastomyces, which favors decomposing leaves/wood and forms large yeast with characteristic broad-based budding.

questions.stem: This fungus is endemic to the arid region shown (notably southern Arizona and California's San Joaquin Valley). The inhaled infectious particles of its mold form take which shape?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Barrel-shaped arthroconidia arranged like a bracelet

explanation.summary: This is the range of Coccidioides (Valley Fever); its mold form fragments into barrel-shaped arthroconidia that are inhaled and then convert to spherules in tissue.
explanation.whyCorrect: This is the range of Coccidioides (Valley Fever); its mold form fragments into barrel-shaped arthroconidia that are inhaled and then convert to spherules in tissue.
